summary: Mathematically healthy proposal — all three filter conditions parse cleanly via supported fields, structurally distinct from existing taker_buy/liquidity archetypes by adding a session gate, with adequate sample size for Phase 3 evaluation.

analyses.distinctness_notes: E126/E064 use taker_buy alone, E051 uses high_liquidity alone; no existing archetype combines session=US with taker_buy_ratio_5m AND liquidity_bucket. The session gate is a genuinely novel regime conditioner — even if taker_buy/liquidity overlap with prior archetypes, the US-hours restriction will materially shift the trigger set and TP/SL surface.
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: 5000 pricechange events × ~0.33 (US session ~8h of 24h, but US hours have above-avg activity so use 0.33 vs naive 0.33) × ~0.35 (taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60 is moderately selective, roughly upper third) × ~0.55 (high liquidity bucket fraction, biased high since US-hour high-volume names dominate) ≈ 5000 × 0.33 × 0.35 × 0.55 ≈ 318. Round to ~340 given US-hour event clustering.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Coverage summary shows fixed-exit family is the ONLY positive-mean family (mean +0.175, max +0.395, 6680/7320 positive); multi_tp/trail/time_only/breakeven all negative-mean. This long-side pricechange pump archetype should pair best with `fixed` (small TP, wide SL avoided per E126 asymmetric-trap lesson) — recommend testing with TP in 0.3–1.0% range and SL ≤2× TP to avoid the R2 left-tail compounding trap. Breakeven family worth a secondary screen since high-liquidity US-hour entries may benefit from quick risk-off, but expect lower expectancy than fixed.
